reasoning: The empirical base rate for ACLED "any increase" questions is 0.225, largely due to the right-skewed nature of protest counts (a few major spikes pull up the 30-day mean over a 360-day period, meaning most 30-day windows fall short of the average). For the past year, Sri Lanka has been under the new left-leaning Dissanayake government, which won a landslide in late 2024, likely depressing the overall 360-day average due to reduced opposition capabilities. However, recent reporting around mid-October 2025 indicates a potential flashpoint with a "presidential arrest" (likely a former outgoing president) and crackdowns on protests in Mannar. This near-term tension elevates the probability of a protest spike crossing the relatively low 360-day line for the immediate November 16 window. Therefore, I predict a higher chance (0.35) for the near term than the generic 0.225 base rate. As the forecast horizon increases (December 2025 and February 2026) and visibility drops, the probabilities artificially revert closer to maximum uncertainty (0.5), pushing P2 and P3 up to 0.38 and 0.42 respectively, in accordance with the instruction that probabilities for distant dates should trend toward 0.5.
